zh.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆
zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh於1087年出生在英國諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆。她和Aldhelm至少有1個女兒。她去世於1108年在她的家鄉享年21歲。

zh-hans.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh于1087年出生在英国諾福爾可波 可特 艾爾善姆。她和Aldhelm至少有1个女儿。她去世于1108年在她的家乡享年21岁。

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh was born in 1087, in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England. She had at least 1 daughter with Aldhelm. She died in 1108, in her hometown, at the age of 21.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh est née en 1087, à Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Aldhelm. Elle est décédée en 1108, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 21 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh wurde 1087 in Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England geboren. Sie hatte mit Aldhelm mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1108 im Alter von 21 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh è nata il nel 1087 nel Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Aldhelm. Morì nel 1108, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 21 anni.

ja.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクブルグ ネクスト エイルシャム
ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Burghは1087年にイギリスノーフォルクブルグ ネクスト エイルシャムで生まれた。彼女はAldhelmとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1108年に故郷で21歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh(은)는 1087년 영국 노르폴크 버그h 니스트 아이샤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Aldhelm(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1108년 그녀의 고향에서 21세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh nasceu em 1087, em Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Aldhelm. Ela faleceu em 1108, em sua cidade natal, com 21 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh родилась в 1087 г. в местности бург некст аилшам, норфолк, Англия. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Aldhelm. Она умерла в 1108 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 21 год.

es.lifeSummary: Agnes Burgh nació en 1087, en Burgh next Aylsham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Aldhelm. Murió en 1108, en su pueblo, a la edad de 21 años.

th.Burgh Next Aylsham, Norfolk, England: เบอรว เนกซต อายลส์ฮัม, เนอรโฟลก, เอิงแลนด์
